description: Diff Viewer Scroll Sync Fix — Replace dual-pane JavaScript scroll synchronization with a single scrollable container so both sides scroll in perfect lockstep with no visual offset.

userStories.title: Single-container scroll for FullDiffView
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Full diff view's left and right panes to scroll perfectly in sync, so that I can compare old and new file content without visual jitter.
acceptanceCriteria: FullDiffView renders both left and right panes inside a single scrollable <div> container instead of two separate scrollable panes
Each row renders as a flex row with a left cell (50% width) and right cell (50% width) side-by-side
Scrolling is handled natively by the single container — no onScroll handler, no scrollTop sync, no syncingRef
Remove leftPaneRef, rightPaneRef, syncingRef, and handleScrollSync from FullDiffView
Pane headers (filename + line count) remain sticky at the top of each column using position: sticky
The vertical scrollbar is on the right edge of the combined container, not per-pane
Line numbers, syntax highlighting, diff coloring (added/removed/context lines), and hunk dividers all render correctly
Hunk action bars (stage/unstage/discard) span the full width of both columns
Line selection checkboxes for staging still work (left pane = removed lines, right pane = added lines)
Binary file and large file placeholders still render correctly
Renamed file header still renders above the panes
Scrollbar diff markers (ScrollbarMarkers component) track the single container's scroll position — update the ref it receives
Typecheck passes, existing tests pass
userStories.notes:
FullDiffView already computes aligned rows via buildFullDiffRows() which returns an array of { left, right, hunkIdx, leftLineIdx, rightLineIdx } objects. Currently these are iterated twice (once per pane). Instead, iterate once and render each row as a
{left cell}
{right cell}
. The parent container gets overflow-y:auto. Each pane's inner div loses overflow-y:auto. The sticky headers need a wrapper row at the top with position:sticky;top:0;z-index:1. For ScrollbarMarkers, pass the single container ref instead of the left pane ref.

userStories.title: Single-container scroll for side-by-side DiffViewer mode
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side-by-side diff view to scroll perfectly in sync, matching the FullDiffView fix.
acceptanceCriteria: The side-by-side mode in DiffViewer renders both panes inside a single scrollable container
Each hunk renders its left and right sides as aligned row pairs — left line and right line on the same flex row
When one side of a hunk has more lines than the other (e.g. 3 removals, 5 additions), blank padding rows fill the shorter side so the next context lines align
Remove leftPaneRef, rightPaneRef, syncingRef, and handleScrollSync from the DiffViewer component
Hunk headers span the full width of both columns
Line numbers and syntax highlighting render correctly on both sides
Word-level diff highlighting still works in side-by-side mode
Scrollbar diff markers track the single container
Typecheck passes, existing tests pass
userStories.notes: The side-by-side DiffViewer currently renders each hunk twice (once in left pane, once in right pane). The new approach: for each hunk, pair up removed lines (left) with added lines (right). Context lines appear on both sides. When the counts differ, pad with empty rows. This is similar to what buildFullDiffRows does for FullDiffView — consider extracting a shared utility or adapting the existing one. The hunk header row should be a full-width flex row with colspan-like behavior.

userStories.title: Cleanup and polish
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove dead scroll sync code and verify no regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: All references to the old scroll sync mechanism are removed from both DiffViewer and FullDiffView
CSS updated: individual panes no longer have overflow-y:auto, the parent container handles scrolling
No visual regressions in inline mode (single column, should be completely unaffected)
File view mode unaffected
Large diff threshold and truncation still work correctly
The DiffViewer mode toggle (Inline / Side-by-side) still switches correctly
Typecheck passes, existing tests pass
userStories.notes: Check the CSS module file DiffViewer.module.css for .sbsPane styles that set overflow-y:auto — these should change to overflow:visible or be removed. Also check .sbsLeft and .sbsView styles. Make sure the parent .sbsView gets overflow-y:auto if it doesn't already. Verify that the inline mode rendering path is completely separate and untouched.
